mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism is always active as a trend signal: lower UNRATE predicts higher stock returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE (negative, since falls predict rises) absolute value < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with UNRATE declines are not significantly higher than in months with increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below the estimated neutral rate (2.5%) indicates accommodative monetary policy that reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations. The signal activates when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% and predicts positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.1% per month) or Rank IC of the binary signal < 0.03 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust demand-pull forces that translate into higher corporate profits and equity prices. The interaction of both proxies rising above trend thresholds confirms the signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS m/m growth > 0.5% and INDPRO m/m growth > 0.3% for the same month
failure_condition: In months when both thresholds are met, 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of occasions, or average return is less than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising from cyclical lows signals recovery in residential investment, a leading indicator of economic expansion. In the post-recession recovery regime, this positively predicts S&P 500 returns but reverses in late-cycle overheating.
failure_condition: In years following the 2008 recession (2009-2014), positive changes in HOUST do not significantly lead positive S&P 500 60-day returns; alternatively, the correlation is not statistically different from zero at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side weakness, often preceding earnings deterioration. A monthly decline of INDPRO triggers a negative signal for near-term equity returns as investors discount lower output.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines month-over-month, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is not negative on average, or the proportion of negative returns is <= 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) pressures real incomes and may prompt monetary tightening, both negative for equity valuations. A monthly CPI increase above 0.2% signals rising inflation that dampens forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when CPI m/m > 0.2%, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment gains (PAYEMS > 200k) combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE declining) provide a joint signal of robust labor market improvement that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving equity prices higher.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m change > 200 (thousands) and UNRATE decreases from previous month
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 60% of instances, or the average return is not > 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals that the economic expansion is maturing and may peak, leading to reduced earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is always active as a trend-following signal.
failure_condition: Negative changes in quarterly GDP growth do not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns significantly (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.
